com.hp.workpath.api.scanner

Class ScannerService

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.scanner.ScannerService

  • public final class ScannerService
extends java.lang.Object

    ScannerService provides an easy to use interface to initiate built-in tasks related to the scanner. In its simplest form, a scanning task can be initiated by simply calling submit(Context, ScanAttributes, ScanletAttributes). For more control of attributes or to present its own UI, ScanletAttributes has to be built prior to calling submit(Context, ScanAttributes, ScanletAttributes).

    A call to submit(Context, ScanAttributes, ScanletAttributes) starts the scanning task. The scanning process is long running and asynchronous by nature, and details of its progress are communicated to the calling entity via a AbstractJobletObserver. The application must create an object of AbstractJobletObserver and register/unregister it based on the component's life cycle, i.e. Activity or Service.

    Depending upon the task (see SupportedTask) being submitted, its completion is communicated with a call to ILetObserver.onComplete(String, JobInfo)

    Selecting Attributes

    For finer control of the attributes passed to submit(Context, ScanAttributes, ScanletAttributes), note that the application must determine the existing capability of the device. These capabilities differ per devices. A call to getCapabilities(Context, Result) returns these capabilities to the caller.

    Submitting a Job

    The following is to show how to select attributes and create a scan job: 

     private static final class ScanAsyncTask extends AsyncTask<Void, Void, Void> {
 
    
 protected Void doInBackground(final Void... params) {
 Result result = new Result();
 final ScanAttributesCaps caps = ScannerService.getCapabilities(
 mContext, result);
 
    
 if (null == caps) {
 // Unable to retrieve capabilities. Need to look at the result to
 // see what happened.
 return null;
 }
 
    
 try {
 final ScanAttributes.MeBuilder meBuilder = new ScanAttributes.MeBuilder();
 
    
 // Scan in color, if available. Otherwise, use the default.
 for (final ColorMode cm : caps.getColorModeList()) {
 if (ColorMode.COLOR.equals(cm)) {
 meBuilder.setColorMode(cm);
 }
 }
 
    
 // Create the scan attributes
 final ScanAttributes scanAttributes = meBuilder.build(caps);
 
    
 // Create the scanlet attributes
 final ScanletAttributes scanletAttributes = new ScanletAttributes.Builder()
 .build();
 
    
 ScannerService.submit(mContext, scanAttributes, scanletAttributes);
 
    
 } catch (final CapabilitiesExceededException cee) {
 // Either an attribute was outside of the capabilities or the
 // capabilities were empty.
 }
 }
 }

    Supported Tasks

    • Scan To Me
    • Scan To HTTP
    • Scan To NetworkFolder
    • Scan To FTP
    • Scan To Email
    • Scan To Usb

    Scan To Me

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and saved in the local storage on the device, where other apps can access them.

    Scan To HTTP

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and sent to the HTTP server which configured by an application.

    Scan To NetworkFolder

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and saved in the shared folder on network.

    Scan To FTP

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and sent to the FTP server which configured by an application.

    Scan To Email

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and sent to email as attachments based on SMTP configuration.

    Scan To Usb

    When this task is deployed, the documents are scanned and sent to Usb which selected by a user.

    Monitoring the Job

    After submitting the job, it can be monitored by using the monitorJobInForeground(Activity, int, JobletAttributes, Intent).

    Canceling the Job

    To cancel a submitted job, use cancelJob(Context, int)
    Since:
    API 1

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static ScanAttributesCaps getCapabilities(android.content.Context context, Result result)

      This synchronous operation returns scan capabilities for particular Transmission Mode.

      static java.lang.String submit(android.content.Context context, ScanAttributes scanAttributes, ScanletAttributes letAttributes)
      Creates and requests a scan job asynchronously to the device using the provided scan attributes.

    • Method Detail

      • submit

        public static java.lang.String submit(android.content.Context context,
                                      ScanAttributes scanAttributes,
                                      ScanletAttributes letAttributes)

        Creates and requests a scan job asynchronously to the device using the provided scan attributes. If AbstractJobletObserver is present, the AbstractJobletObserver will receive call backs indicating job progress, completion and errors.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        scanAttributes - Attributes related to scan.

        ScanAttributes default values: The default value can be figured out by getDefaults(Context, Result).

        • ColorMode = ScanAttributes.ColorMode.COLOR
        • Duplex = ScanAttributes.Duplex.NONE
        • Destination = ME
        • DocumentFormat = ScanAttributes.DocumentFormat.JPEG
        • ScanSize = ScanAttributes.ScanSize.LETTER
        • Resolution = ScanAttributes.Resolution.DPI_300
        • Orientation = ScanAttributes.Orientation.PORTRAIT
        • ScanPreview = ScanAttributes.ScanPreview.FALSE
        • FileName = null
        • CredentialsUsername = null
        • CredentialsPassword = null
        • BackgroundCleanup = ScanAttributes.BackgroundCleanup.LEVEL_2
        • ContrastAdjustment = ScanAttributes.ContrastAdjustment.LEVEL_4
        • DarknessAdjustment = ScanAttributes.DarknessAdjustment.LEVEL_4
        • BlankImageRemovalMode = ScanAttributes.BlankImageRemovalMode.OFF
        • ColorDropoutMode = ScanAttributes.ColorDropoutMode.OFF
        • CropMode = ScanAttributes.CropMode.OFF
        • ProgressDialogMode = ScanAttributes.ProgressDialogMode.OFF
        • OutputQuality = ScanAttributes.OutputQuality.MEDIUM
        • TransmissionMode = ScanAttributes.TransmissionMode.JOB
        • JobAssemblyMode = ScanAttributes.JobAssemblyMode.OFF
        • SharpnessAdjustment = ScanAttributes.SharpnessAdjustment.LEVEL_2
        • MediaWeightAdjustment = ScanAttributes.MediaWeightAdjustment.NORMAL
        • TextPhotoOptimization = ScanAttributes.TextPhotoOptimization.MIXED_2
        • MediaSource = ScanAttributes.MediaSource.AUTO
        • MisfeedDetectionMode = ScanAttributes.MisfeedDetectionMode.OFF
        • CustomLength = 0.0f
        • CustomWidth = 0.0f
        • SplitAttachmentByPage = ScanAttributes.SplitAttachmentByPage.DISABLED
        • MaxPagesPerAttachment = 9999
        • EraseMarginUnit = ScanAttributes.EraseMarginUnit.INCHES
        • EraseBackBottom = 0.0f
        • EraseBackLeft = 0.0f
        • EraseBackRight = 0.0f
        • EraseBackTop = 0.0f
        • EraseFrontBottom = 0.0f
        • EraseFrontLeft = 0.0f
        • EraseFrontRight = 0.0f
        • EraseFrontTop = 0.0f
        • CaptureMode = ScanAttributes.CaptureMode.STANDARD
        • AutomaticToneMode = ScanAttributes.AutomaticToneMode.DISABLE
        • AutomaticStraightenMode = ScanAttributes.AutomaticStraightenMode.DISABLE

        letAttributes - Attributes related to how the ScannerService should behave.
        Returns:
        Request id which is used to correspond AbstractJobletObserver callbacks with the original submit.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context or ScanAttributes are null with disabled Show Setting UI
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if incompatible attributes are provided (e.g. ScanAttributes has Settings UI false and ScanAttributes are null)
        Since:
        API 1

      • getCapabilities

        public static ScanAttributesCaps getCapabilities(android.content.Context context,
                                                 Result result)

        This synchronous operation returns the intersection of device and ScannerService capabilities. In this example, for the color mode attribute:

        • The color modes supported from the device are {mono, auto}
        • The color modes of the ScannerService are {mono, color}

        Therefore, the color mode capabilities will include only {mono}.

        Note that for some attributes, the intersection of values may result in empty capabilities.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving capabilities.
        Returns:
        Capabilities found both on the Printer and in the ScannerService or null.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If context is null.
        Since:
        API 1

      • getCapabilities

        public static ScanAttributesCaps getCapabilities(android.content.Context context,
                                                 ScanAttributes.TransmissionMode transmissionMode,
                                                 Result result)

        This synchronous operation returns scan capabilities for particular Transmission Mode. Capabilities can vary for ScanAttributes.TransmissionMode.IMAGE and ScanAttributes.TransmissionMode.JOB. E.g. for ScanAttributes.TransmissionMode.IMAGE only limited ScanAttributes.DocumentFormat can be supported.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        transmissionMode - Transmission mode to get capabilities for
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving capabilities.
        Returns:
        Capabilities found both on the Printer and in the ScannerService or null.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context or transmissionMode is null.
        Since:
        API 1

      • getDefaults

        public static ScanAttributes getDefaults(android.content.Context context,
                                         Result result)
        Retrieves the device default values for scan attributes. If the value is DEFAULT, it means it is not supported attribute from the device.
        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving defaults.
        Returns:
        ScanAttributes containing default values for submitting a job
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context is null.
        Since:
        API 1

      • getFileOptionsCapabilities

        public static FileOptionsAttributesCaps getFileOptionsCapabilities(android.content.Context context,
                                                                   ScanAttributes.ColorMode colorMode,
                                                                   ScanAttributes.DocumentFormat documentFormat,
                                                                   Result result)

        This synchronous operation returns file options capabilities for particular pair of Color Mode and Document Format.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        colorMode - ColorMode to get capabilities for
        documentFormat - DocumentFormat to get capabilities for
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving capabilities.
        Returns:
        FileOptionsAttributesCaps file options capabilities
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context, colorMode or documentFormat are null.
        Since:
        API 1

      • isSupported

        public static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)

        This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not. If it's not supported, ScannerService operation will be failed.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        Returns:
        boolean Returns result of supported. If service is supported, method returns true.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if context is null.
        Since:
        API 1
